steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find tan(Y) from the right triangle formed by the altitude
steps.content:
The altitude $WX$ from the right angle vertex $X$ to the hypotenuse $YZ$ creates a right triangle $XWY$ (right angle at $W$). In this triangle:
$$\tan(Y) = \frac{WX}{YW} = \frac{459}{612} = \frac{3}{4}$$

steps.title: Apply complementary angles to find tan(Z)
steps.content:
In right triangle $XYZ$ with right angle at $X$, angles $Y$ and $Z$ are complementary ($Y + Z = 90^{\circ}$). Therefore:
$$\tan(Z) = \frac{1}{\tan(Y)} = \frac{1}{\frac{3}{4}} = \frac{4}{3}$$
That confirms A is correct.
